nodes.name: Billboard #1 Hits
nodes.description: The most iconic chart-topping singles in Billboard history

items.prompt: Which 1956 double-sided single spent 11 combined weeks atop the charts, with one side about a lazy canine and the other warning 'don't be mean to me'?
items.explanation:
"Hound Dog" / "Don't Be Cruel" (1956) topped the pre-Hot 100 charts for a combined 11 weeks
Biggest double-sided hit of the 1950s — one side reached #1, the other peaked at #2
First record to top all three Billboard singles charts simultaneously: Pop, R&B, and Country
Originally written by Leiber and Stoller for Big Mama Thornton, who recorded it in 1952
"Don't Be Cruel" written by Otis Blackwell, who also co-wrote "All Shook Up" and "Great Balls of Fire"
Predates the Billboard Hot 100, which launched in August 1958
Sold over 4 million copies in its first year of release
items.cardFront: 1950s — double-sided #1 about a canine + cruelty (Artist?)

items.prompt: Which 1964 single spent 7 weeks at #1 on the Hot 100 and is credited with launching a musical 'invasion' from across the Atlantic?
items.explanation:
"I Want to Hold Your Hand" (1964) spent 7 weeks at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100
First Hot 100 #1 for the group — debuted on the chart January 18, 1964, and reached #1 by February 1
Launched the British Invasion of American pop music
Ed Sullivan Show appearance on February 9, 1964, drew 73 million viewers
Song was knocked from #1 by the band's own "She Loves You"
First of 20 #1 singles on the Hot 100 — still the most by any act
Written by Lennon–McCartney in the basement of the home of Jane Asher (McCartney's girlfriend)
items.cardFront: 1964 — 7 weeks at #1, sparked the British Invasion (Artist?)
items.cardBack: "I Want to Hold Your Hand" — The Beatles

items.prompt: Which 1970 gospel-tinged ballad about offering comfort spent 6 weeks at #1 and was the #1 song on the Billboard Year-End chart that year, released by a folk duo on the verge of splitting?
items.explanation:
Simon & Garfunkel (1970) — 6 weeks at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 beginning February 28
#1 on the Billboard Year-End Hot 100 for 1970
Album of the same name won Grammy for Album of the Year (1971) and sold over 25 million copies worldwide
Art Garfunkel sang lead; Paul Simon later said he regretted giving away his best song
Released just months before Simon & Garfunkel's breakup
Produced by Roy Halee at Columbia Studios in New York
Inspired by a line from a gospel song by the Swan Silvertones: "I'll be your bridge over deep water"
items.cardFront: 1970 — gospel-tinged folk ballad, 6 weeks at #1, duo split shortly after (Song?)

items.prompt: Which 1978 disco anthem from a blockbuster film soundtrack spent 4 weeks at #1 and later became the recommended tempo for performing CPR chest compressions?
items.explanation:
Bee Gees (1978) — 4 weeks at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 beginning February 4
From the *Saturday Night Fever* soundtrack, which sold over 40 million copies worldwide
BPM of ~103 matches the recommended rate for CPR chest compressions (100–120 per minute)
One of three consecutive Bee Gees #1 singles from the film (with "How Deep Is Your Love" and "Night Fever")
Brothers Barry, Robin, and Maurice Gibb wrote and performed the song
The *Saturday Night Fever* soundtrack spent 24 weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200
Bee Gees wrote or produced all six #1 hits from the film soundtrack
items.cardFront: 1978 — disco anthem from a film soundtrack, CPR training staple (Song?)

items.prompt: Which 1977 single about checking into a mysterious desert lodging spent just 1 week at #1 on the Hot 100 but became one of the most iconic rock songs ever recorded?
items.explanation:
Eagles (1977) — peaked at #1 for 1 week on the Billboard Hot 100 (May 7, 1977)
Knocked from #1 by Leo Sayer's "When I Need You" after a single week
Title track from the Eagles' fifth studio album, which spent 8 non-consecutive weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200
Don Felder wrote the music; Don Henley and Glenn Frey wrote the lyrics
Famous dual guitar outro solo by Felder and Joe Walsh — consistently ranked among greatest guitar solos
Lyrics widely interpreted as a metaphor for hedonism, the dark side of the American Dream, or the music industry
Single released on Asylum Records on February 22, 1977
items.cardFront: 1977 — mysterious desert lodging, only 1 week at #1 but iconic (Song?)

items.prompt: Which 1983 hit about obsessive surveillance spent 8 weeks at #1 and was the biggest-selling single of that year, performed by a British trio on their final studio album?
alternates: Every Breath You Take by the Police
items.explanation:
The Police (1983) — 8 weeks at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 beginning July 9
Written by Sting, who later said the lyrics are about jealousy and surveillance, not romance
From the album *Synchronicity*, the band's fifth and final studio album
Biggest-selling single of 1983 in the United States
Only #1 hit on the Hot 100 for The Police
Won the Grammy for Song of the Year in 1984
Sampled by Puff Daddy in "I'll Be Missing You" (1997), a tribute to the Notorious B.I.G. that also hit #1
items.cardFront: 1983 — obsessive surveillance anthem, 8 weeks at #1, British trio's final album (Song?)

items.prompt: Which artist's 1983 single about a paternity claim spent 7 weeks at #1, and its first televised performance debuted a dance move that became one of pop culture's most recognizable?
items.explanation:
"Billie Jean" (1983) — 7 weeks at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 beginning March 5
From the album *Thriller*, the best-selling album of all time (70+ million copies)
First performance of the moonwalk on *Motown 25: Yesterday, Today, Forever* (March 25, 1983)
First Black artist to have a video in heavy rotation on MTV ("Billie Jean" broke the color barrier)
Produced by Quincy Jones; distinctive bassline by Louis Johnson
First Black artist to have a video in heavy rotation on MTV, breaking the network's de facto color barrier
#1 song on the Billboard Year-End chart for 1983
items.cardFront: 1983 — paternity claim, moonwalk debut, 7 weeks at #1 (Artist?)

items.prompt: Which 1984 single, notable for having no bass line in its final mix, spent 5 weeks at #1 and was the #1 single of the year from the soundtrack of a semi-autobiographical film?
alternates: The Artist Formerly Known as Prince
items.explanation:
"When Doves Cry" (1984) — 5 weeks at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 beginning July 7
#1 single on the Billboard Year-End Hot 100 for 1984
From the *Purple Rain* soundtrack and film — album spent 24 weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200
Famously has no bass line; the bass track was stripped out during mixing because the artist felt it sounded too conventional
First #1 single for the Minneapolis-born multi-instrumentalist
Music video featured the artist in a bathtub and incorporated footage from the film
Won the American Music Award for Best Black Single in 1985
items.cardFront: 1984 — no bass line, 5 weeks at #1, semi-autobiographical film soundtrack (Artist?)

items.prompt: Which 1984 single, famously performed at the inaugural MTV VMAs in a wedding dress, spent 6 weeks at #1 and became the debut chart-topper for the artist later called the 'Queen of Pop'?
items.explanation:
Madonna (1984–1985) — 6 weeks at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 beginning December 22, 1984
First #1 hit for the artist who would earn 12 total Hot 100 #1 singles
Performed at the first-ever MTV Video Music Awards (September 14, 1984) in a wedding dress atop a giant cake
Written by Billy Steinberg and Tom Kelly, not by the performer
Title track of the album that established the singer as a global superstar (sold 21+ million copies)
Produced by Nile Rodgers of Chic
Longest-running #1 single of 1984 on the Hot 100
items.cardFront: 1984 — wedding dress at VMAs, 6 weeks at #1, 'Queen of Pop' (Song?)

items.prompt: Which 1992 power ballad, a cover of a 1973 country song written by the original artist for her mentor, spent 14 weeks at #1 and appeared on a blockbuster film soundtrack starring the singer alongside Kevin Costner?
items.explanation:
"I Will Always Love You" (1992) — 14 weeks at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 beginning November 28
Originally written and recorded by Dolly Parton in 1974 as a farewell to her mentor Porter Wagoner
From *The Bodyguard* soundtrack, which sold over 45 million copies worldwide
Set the record for most weeks at #1 by a female artist at the time
Won Grammys for Record of the Year and Best Pop Vocal Performance, Female (1994)
Fastest rise to #1 by a female artist at the time — reached #1 in its second week on the chart
Parton earned millions in royalties from the cover and reportedly thanked the singer for her "house money"
items.cardFront: 1992 — Dolly Parton cover, 14 weeks at #1, *The Bodyguard* (Artist?)
items.cardBack: "I Will Always Love You" — Whitney Houston

items.prompt: Which 1995 collaboration between a diva and a vocal quartet debuted at #1 and held the top spot for 16 weeks, a record that stood for over two decades?
items.explanation:
Mariah Carey & Boyz II Men (1995–1996) — 16 weeks at #1 from December 2, 1995, to March 16, 1996
Debuted at #1 on the Hot 100 — longest-running #1 debut in chart history
Held the record for most weeks at #1 for 23 years until "Old Town Road" (19 consecutive weeks) in 2019
Written as a tribute to friends who had died, including AIDS-related deaths
Carey's 10th #1 single (of her eventual 19 total); Boyz II Men's 4th
Produced by Walter Afanasieff and Carey
Record was eventually surpassed in total (non-consecutive) weeks at #1 by "All I Want for Christmas Is You" (22 weeks as of 2025)
items.cardFront: 1995 — diva + vocal quartet, debuted at #1, held 16-week record (Song?)
items.cardBack: "One Sweet Day" — Mariah Carey & Boyz II Men

items.prompt: Which 1995 hip-hop single, built on a Stevie Wonder sample, spent 3 weeks at #1 and was the top-selling single of the year, featured on the soundtrack of a Michelle Pfeiffer school drama?
items.explanation:
Coolio feat. L.V. (1995) — 3 weeks at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100
#1 single on the Billboard Year-End Hot 100 for 1995
From the *Dangerous Minds* soundtrack, starring Michelle Pfeiffer as a teacher in an inner-city school
Sampled Stevie Wonder's "Pastime Paradise" from *Songs in the Key of Life* (1976); Wonder required that no profanity appear in the song
Spent 12 weeks in the Hot 100 top two (3 at #1, 9 at #2)
Won the Grammy for Best Rap Solo Performance in 1996
Coolio (Artis Leon Ivey Jr.) died in 2022 at age 59 from cardiac arrest
items.cardFront: 1995 — Stevie Wonder sample, *Dangerous Minds*, top-selling single of the year (Song?)

items.prompt: Which 1999 debut single, featuring a now-iconic Catholic schoolgirl outfit in its music video, spent 2 weeks at #1 and launched the career of a 17-year-old pop sensation?
items.explanation:
"...Baby One More Time" (1999) — 2 weeks at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 beginning January 30
Debuted at #1 on both the Hot 100 and the Billboard 200 simultaneously — rare for any artist
Launched by a 17-year-old former Mickey Mouse Club member from Kentwood, Louisiana
Music video set in a school hallway with a Catholic schoolgirl outfit became a defining pop culture image of the late 1990s
Written and produced by Max Martin, the Swedish hitmaker behind dozens of #1 singles
Album of the same name sold over 25 million copies worldwide
Marked the beginning of the late-1990s teen pop boom alongside *NSYNC and the Backstreet Boys
items.cardFront: 1999 — schoolgirl video, 2 weeks at #1, teen pop debut (Artist?)

items.prompt: Which 2003 single, featuring a horn-driven beat and a rap verse from the performer's future husband, spent 8 weeks at #1 and marked a former girl-group member's solo breakthrough?
items.explanation:
Beyoncé feat. Jay-Z (2003) — 8 weeks at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 beginning July 12
First solo #1 for the former Destiny's Child frontwoman (she would earn 10 total)
Produced by Rich Harrison; the iconic horn sample comes from the Chi-Lites' "Are You My Woman (Tell Me So)" (1970)
From the debut solo album *Dangerously in Love*, which won 5 Grammys
Jay-Z and Beyoncé married in 2008 and became one of music's most powerful couples
Won the Grammy for Best R&B Song and Best Rap/Sung Collaboration (2004)
Music video's opening — walking toward an exploding car — became an iconic pop culture moment
items.cardFront: 2003 — horn-driven beat, rap verse from future husband, 8 weeks at #1 (Song?)

items.prompt: Which 2003 single spent 9 weeks at #1 and instructed listeners to 'shake it like a Polaroid picture,' released by one half of an Atlanta hip-hop duo as a solo track?
items.explanation:
OutKast / André 3000 (2003–2004) — 9 weeks at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 beginning December 13
Technically credited to OutKast but performed solely by André 3000 (André Benjamin)
From the double album *Speakerboxxx/The Love Below* — each disc by one half of the duo
"Shake it like a Polaroid picture" became a catchphrase; Polaroid issued a statement saying shaking actually damages their photos
*Speakerboxxx/The Love Below* won the Grammy for Album of the Year (2004)
Blended pop, funk, punk, and New Wave in a genre-defying package
Music video featured André 3000 playing all members of a fictional band in a parody of 1960s variety shows
items.cardFront: 2003 — 'shake it like a Polaroid picture,' 9 weeks at #1, Atlanta duo (Song?)

items.prompt: Which 2011 single, driven by a stomping beat and soaring vocals about a failed relationship, spent 7 weeks at #1 and became the top-selling single of the year for a British singer's second album?
items.explanation:
"Rolling in the Deep" (2011) — 7 weeks at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 beginning May 21
#1 song on the Billboard Year-End Hot 100 for 2011
From the album *21*, which sold over 31 million copies worldwide and spent 24 weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200
Won three Grammys: Record of the Year, Song of the Year, and Best Short Form Music Video (2012)
*21* swept all six of its Grammy categories at the 2012 ceremony, including Album of the Year
Co-written with Paul Epworth; inspired by the breakup with the singer's ex-boyfriend
First single by the British artist to reach #1 in the United States
items.cardFront: 2011 — stomping beat, breakup anthem, 7 weeks at #1, British singer (Artist?)

items.prompt: Which 2015 retro single, channeling James Brown-era grooves, spent 14 weeks at #1, was credited to a British-born producer but performed vocally by a Hawaiian-born singer, and became the #1 song of the year?
items.explanation:
Mark Ronson feat. Bruno Mars (2015) — 14 consecutive weeks at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 beginning January 17
#1 song on the Billboard Year-End Hot 100 for 2015
Officially credited to producer Mark Ronson (British, born in London) with Bruno Mars (born Peter Gene Hernandez in Honolulu)
Inspired by 1980s funk and Minneapolis sound — nods to The Gap Band, Zapp, and Morris Day and The Time
Won the Grammy for Record of the Year (2016)
Music video set in various locations inspired by 1980s Black music culture
Became one of the first songs to surpass 4 billion views on YouTube
items.cardFront: 2015 — retro-funk, British producer + Hawaiian singer, 14 weeks at #1 (Song?)
items.cardBack: "Uptown Funk" — Mark Ronson feat. Bruno Mars

items.prompt: Which 2017 remix, blending reggaeton with a Canadian pop star's verse, tied the 16-week #1 record and became the first primarily Spanish-language song to top the Hot 100 since 'Macarena'?
items.explanation:
Luis Fonsi & Daddy Yankee feat. Justin Bieber (2017) — 16 weeks at #1 on the Hot 100 beginning May 27
Tied "One Sweet Day" for the most weeks at #1 at the time (record later broken by "Old Town Road")
First primarily Spanish-language song to reach #1 on the Hot 100 since Los del Río's "Macarena" in 1996
Original version (without Bieber) was already a massive Latin hit before the English-Spanish remix went global
Bieber learned his Spanish-language verses phonetically
Became the first video to reach 5 billion views on YouTube (later surpassed)
Luis Fonsi is from Puerto Rico; Daddy Yankee is the "King of Reggaeton"
items.cardFront: 2017 — reggaeton remix, Canadian pop star verse, tied 16-week #1 record (Song?)

items.prompt: Which 2017 marimba-driven pop hit spent 12 weeks at #1 and set the record for most weeks in the Hot 100's top 10 (33 weeks), released by a British singer-songwriter known for performing with just a guitar and loop pedal?
items.explanation:
"Shape of You" (2017) — 12 non-consecutive weeks at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 beginning January 28
Set the record for most weeks in the Hot 100 top 10 at the time (33 weeks)
From the album *÷ (Divide)*, which debuted at #1 in virtually every major market
Co-written with Steve Mac and Johnny McDaid; originally intended for Rihanna
Debuted at #1 on the Hot 100 alongside "Castle on the Hill" at #6 — unprecedented double debut
British singer-songwriter from Halifax, Suffolk, known for solo live shows using loop pedals
Became Apple Music's most-streamed song of the 2010s decade
items.cardFront: 2017 — marimba-driven pop, 12 weeks at #1, loop-pedal performer (Artist?)

items.prompt: Which 2019 country-rap mashup spent a record 19 consecutive weeks at #1, initially went viral on TikTok, and sparked controversy when it was briefly removed from the Billboard country chart?
alternates: Old Town Road by Lil Nas X
items.explanation:
Lil Nas X feat. Billy Ray Cyrus (2019) — 19 consecutive weeks at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100
Held the record for most total weeks at #1 until surpassed by Mariah Carey's "All I Want for Christmas Is You" (22 non-consecutive weeks as of 2025)
Still holds the record for most consecutive weeks at #1 (19)
Originally removed from the Billboard Hot Country Songs chart, sparking debate about genre gatekeeping and race
Billy Ray Cyrus remix was released April 5, 2019, after the original had already charted
Lil Nas X (Montero Lamar Hill) was 20 years old and had bought the beat online for $30
Went viral on TikTok via the "Yeehaw Challenge" before mainstream radio pickup
items.cardFront: 2019 — country-rap TikTok viral hit, 19 consecutive weeks at #1 (Song?)
items.cardBack: "Old Town Road" — Lil Nas X feat. Billy Ray Cyrus
